subject: Geography
chapter: The World Population Distribution, Density and Growth

concepts.title: Global Population
concepts.description: The global population is approximately 7.9 billion (2020 estimates).

concepts.title: Population Density Criteria
concepts.description: Population density is classified into three categories: Less than 100 people per sq km (Low), 100-400 people per sq km (Moderate), and more than 400 people per sq km (High).

concepts.title: Factors Influencing Population Growth
concepts.description: The factors influencing population growth are fertility rate, mortality rate, and migration rate.

concepts.title: Population Pyramid
concepts.description: To remember the structure of a population pyramid: 'Base represents the youth, and the top represents the elderly'.

concepts.title: Confusing Birth and Death Rates
concepts.description: Students often confuse birth rate and death rate. Birth rate is the number of live births per 1000 population per year, while death rate is the number of deaths per 1000 population per year.

concepts.title: Population Terms
concepts.description: Key terms to remember: Population (total number of people), Density (people per sq km), Growth rate (annual percentage increase), Fertility rate (average number of children born per woman), and Mortality rate (death rate per 1000 population per year).

concepts.title: Urbanization Rate
concepts.description: The urbanization rate is the percentage of the population living in urban areas. India's urbanization rate is around 34% (2020 estimates).

concepts.title: Population Growth Rate
concepts.description: The population growth rate is a measure of the rate of change in a country's population over a specific period. It is expressed as a percentage.
